title: About
text:
I’m a Statistics & Data Science student at UCLA with a strong interest in quantitative analysis, finance, and data-driven storytelling. My academic and project work has helped me build a foundation in statistical modeling, inference, machine learning, and experimental design, while also showing me the importance of clear communication and context.
What draws me most to this field is the ability to turn complex information into insight. I enjoy the full process of analytical work: asking the right questions, cleaning and understanding the data, selecting methods carefully, and presenting results in a way that is useful and persuasive. For me, strong analysis is not only about technical correctness, but also about clarity, structure, and impact.
Beyond coursework, I’ve explored this through internships, research-based projects, and leadership experiences. My work has included credit risk and data science at GoTo, public-facing digital humanities research, and student-led projects in statistical learning and applied inference. These experiences have strengthened both my technical skills and my interest in solving real-world problems with data.
I’m especially interested in opportunities at the intersection of analytics, finance, and decision-making. Looking ahead, I hope to continue building work that combines quantitative rigor with strong communication, whether in risk, strategy, data science, or broader analytical roles.

title: About
text:

title: Statistics, data science, and quantitative analysis for real-world problems.
text:
Victoria Vivian Chan




UCLA Statistics & Data Science student building analytical, research-driven, and data-focused work across machine learning, statistical modeling, and data storytelling.


I am interested in applying statistical and computational methods to understand complex datasets and support data-driven decision making. My work ranges from statistical learning and experimental design to digital humanities and applied analytics, with growing interests in quantitative finance, risk, and strategy.

title: Statistics, data science, and quantitative analysis for real-world problems.
text:
Welcome
I am a UCLA Statistics and Data Science student interested in statistical modeling, machine learning, data analysis, and research communication. I enjoy building work that combines technical rigor with clarity, whether that means developing predictive models, designing experiments, or presenting insights through polished reports and public-facing websites.
Across coursework, internships, and independent projects, I have worked on problems involving classification, inference, visualization, and applied analytics. I am especially interested in opportunities where strong analytical methods can support better decision making.

title: Experience
text:
Data Science Intern — GoTo


2025

Worked on credit risk and model-monitoring related tasks, including performance analysis, drift checks, and production-oriented data workflows. Supported analytical work that connected modeling, business risk, and real-world deployment.



 Corporate Development Intern — GoTo


2024

Supported strategic and market research projects through analysis of industry trends, financial materials, and business reports. Contributed to presentations and research used for broader corporate decision-making.



 President — Indonesian Bruin Student Association


UCLA

Led student programming, planning, and coordination efforts while helping strengthen community engagement on campus. Worked across teams to organize events, manage responsibilities, and support club initiatives.



 Data Journalist — Bruin Sports Analytics


UCLA

Created sports-focused analytical content that combined data, storytelling, and visual communication. Used quantitative methods to explore patterns in performance and communicate findings in an engaging way.

title: Experience
text:
Tools
R, Python, SQL, Excel, Kubeflow, QuickBI, Quarto, and data visualization workflows for research, reporting, and presentation.

title: Statistics, data science, and quantitative analysis for real-world problems.
text:
Featured Work



Predicting US Skin Cancer Status


Machine learning and statistical classification

Built and compared classification models on more than 50,000 observations to predict benign versus malignant skin cancer status.



Beyond the Board


Digital humanities and public-facing research

Created a website exploring structural inequality in education through narrative writing, historical framing, and data storytelling.



Music, Exercise Intensity, and Performance


Experimental design and statistical inference

Used factorial ANOVA to study how music and exercise intensity influence running performance and heart rate.
